subject: Product Management
title: Sean Ellis's 40% Test
description: A fast breakdown of Sean Ellis's 40% Test for builders.

hook.text: How do you know if you have PMF? Sean Ellis proposed a dead-simple survey with one question.

buildup.text: Ask users: 'How would you feel if you could no longer use this product?' with three options: disappointed, somewhat, not at all.

discovery.text: If 40% or more say 'very disappointed,' you have product-market fit. Below 40%, you're still searching.

twist.text: Ellis tested this against hundreds of startups. The 40% threshold reliably predicted which ones would grow.

climax.text: Superhuman used this exact survey to measure and systematically improve their PMF score from 22% to over 58%.

punchline.text: One question. One number. 40% means you've arrived.

quiz.question: What is the Sean Ellis test for product-market fit?
options: If 40% of users would be 'very disappointed' without the product, you have PMF
If 40% of users recommend the product to friends
If 40% of users pay for the premium version
